text.title: Fast vs Slow Burn Rule Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.description: Every burn produces a page; on-call burnt out; pages dismissed without action.
antipattern.note: Fix: Replace with 4-window ruleset. Calibrate thresholds. Route 3d/30d to ticket/review (no page).

antipattern.description: Engineer woken at 3 AM for a 30-day trend alert.
antipattern.reason: All windows routed to same paging receiver.
antipattern.note: Fix: Split routing: 1h/6h → page; 3d → ticket; 30d → review channel.

antipattern.description: Alert fires; on-call doesn't know what to do; scrambles.
antipattern.note: Fix: Per-window runbook. Add annotation. Test by triggering alert in staging.

antipattern.description: SLO improved from 99% to 99.9%; alerts still fire at old threshold; false negatives.
antipattern.note: Fix: Use SLO-relative burn rate. Quarterly recompute on SLO change. Redeploy alerts.
